summary: Julia Evans announces she's leaving her job to spend a full year working on explaining computer things. After six years of blogging, 11 zines, 370,000 words, and 20 talks, she's turning her passion for technical education into a full-time endeavor. She plans to write more zines and build interactive learning tools. She'll run it as a solo business, which is already generating enough revenue to live on, without plans to hire employees or take on consulting work.
key_points: After 6 years of blogging and creating zines as a side project, she's going full-time on explaining computer concepts
Her plan includes writing up to 10 zines in a year and building interactive learning tools that let people learn by breaking things
Her existing zine business already earns more than her first programming job, making the leap financially viable
She's deliberately keeping the operation small — no employees, no consulting, no world domination plans
She expects the blog to shift from 'here's what I'm learning at work' to 'here are explanations of things I mostly already know'
money_quotes: Sometimes there are things you just feel compelled to do.
I've written 11 zines, 370,000 words on this blog, and given 20 or so talks. So it seems like I like explaining things a lot.
I learn things best by trying things out and breaking them, so I want to see if I can facilitate that a little bit for other people.
This business has been making more than I made in my first programming job (that is, definitely enough money to live on!), which has been really surprising and great.
I also don't have any specific plans for world domination or to work 80-hour weeks.
It's been pretty interesting to learn more about running a small business and so far I like it more than I thought I would. (except for taxes, which I like exactly as much as I thought I would)
key_insight: After years of explaining computer things as a side project, turning it into a sustainable solo business feels like a natural next step when the work is something you feel compelled to do.
